normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU1_precondition (t : Tree) (h : IsBst t) : IsBst t := h
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input tree satisfies the BST precondition (in-order traversal is strictly ascending).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU2_bst_invariant_preserved (t : Tree) (k : Int) (h : IsBst t) : IsBst (bstInsert t k) := by
  -- proof obligation: bstInsert must preserve the BST invariant
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The output of bstInsert is also a valid BST (in-order traversal remains strictly ascending).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_keyset_change_when_absent (t : Tree) (k : Int) (hbst : IsBst t)
    (hk : k ∉ keys t) : keys (bstInsert t k) = k :: keys t := by
  -- proof obligation: if k is absent, insertion adds exactly one occurrence
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the key k is not already present, the output key multiset equals the input keys with k added (exactly one new occurrence).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU3_keyset_change_when_present (t : Tree) (k : Int) (hbst : IsBst t)
    (hk : k ∈ keys t) : keys (bstInsert t k) = keys t := by
  -- proof obligation: if k is present, insertion is a no-op on the key multiset
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the key k is already present, the output key multiset equals the input key multiset (no new occurrence is added).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU4_duplicate_noop (t : Tree) (k : Int) (hbst : IsBst t)
    (hk : k ∈ keys t) : keys (bstInsert t k) = keys t := by
  -- duplicate insertion returns unchanged key multiset
  simpa using SU3_keyset_change_when_present (t := t) (k := k) hbst hk
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Inserting an existing key returns a tree with the same key multiset (duplicate insertion is a no-op).
